objective: Test whether Chavez symmetry on spacing ratios r_n = min(g_n, g_{n+1}) / max(g_n, g_{n+1}) produces directionally correct GUE > actual > Poisson ordering

actual_zeros.note: Low-height regime (zeros 1-99, imaginary parts 14-237) — not fully asymptotic GUE

gue_wigner_mean225.distribution: P(s) = (32/pi^2) * s^2 * exp(-4s^2/pi), scaled to mean=2.25

actual_vs_gue_chavez.result: Actual below GUE — expected for low-height regime

ordering.mean_ratio: GUE (0.640) > actual (0.610) > Poisson (0.397) — correct theoretical order
ordering.chavez_symmetry: GUE (76.9%) > actual (75.0%) > Poisson (71.6%) — correct theoretical order
ordering.note: First experiment in series where Chavez produces directionally correct theoretically ordered ranking across all three sequence types

F1_mean_ratio_gue_fingerprint.significance: Actual zeros (0.610) essentially match GUE theoretical expectation (~0.60) and are far above Poisson (~0.39). This confirms Montgomery-Dyson: actual zero gaps have GUE-consistent consecutive regularity. This result requires no Chavez — arithmetic alone suffices.

F2_chavez_directionally_correct.answer: Chavez + spacing ratio pipeline produces correct GUE > actual > Poisson ordering
F2_chavez_directionally_correct.significance: First experiment where Chavez achieves directionally correct ranking. GUE/Poisson separation = 5.3 pts (just clears 5-pt threshold). Chavez has a sensitivity window for GUE statistics when applied to ordering-dependent derived statistics, not raw gap values.

F3_actual_below_gue_expected.answer: Actual zeros (75.0%) score below GUE synthetic (76.9%) on Chavez
F3_actual_below_gue_expected.significance: Physically expected: zeros 1-99 are in the low-height regime, not the asymptotic GUE regime. Phase 5b will test zeros 500-599 where asymptotic GUE behavior is better established.

new_contribution: Chavez conjugation symmetry on spacing ratios achieves GUE/Poisson separation and directionally correct ordering. Not previously documented in literature. Complements the known Montgomery-Dyson result using a novel algebraic tool.
phase5_priorities: Scale test: spacing ratio analysis at n=249, 499, 999 gaps — does GUE/Poisson Chavez separation widen?
Higher zeros: repeat at zeros 500-599 (asymptotic GUE regime) — actual zeros should score closer to GUE synthetic
AT-1 annihilation topology: independent, can run in parallel
